title: Sour Juicy Norwegian Duck Legs Stew
description: A traditional Norwegian stew featuring tender duck legs in a sour and savory broth, perfect for a cold monsoon day. The dish is fermented to give it a unique flavor profile.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together sour cream, buttermilk, aquavit, caraway seeds, juniper berries, salt, and pepper.
Add the duck legs to the marinade and mix well to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 days or up to 4 days.
Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. Remove the duck legs from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Sear the duck legs until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the duck legs from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onions to the pot and cook until they are softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil. Return the duck legs to the pot and simmer, covered, until the meat is tender and falling off the bone, about 1 1/2 hours.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly. Serve the duck legs with the sour and savory broth spooned over the top.
Garnish with fresh herbs, such as parsley or thyme, if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze the stew for later use. Reheat the stew to an internal temperature of at least 165°F (74°C) before serving.
